stories.title: Andy Burnham Set to Become Next Prime Minister Next Week
stories.summary: Andy Burnham, MP for Makerfield, has secured the backing of 349 Labour MPs, making it impossible for any other candidate to challenge him for the Labour leadership. He is on track to become the UK's next Prime Minister within days.

stories.title: Olly Robbins Launches Legal Challenge to Dismissal from Foreign Office
stories.summary: Former FCDO top civil servant Olly Robbins has launched a legal challenge to his dismissal, claiming he was given 'irrational' reasons for being fired amid the Peter Mandelson vetting scandal.

stories.title: Burnham's First Reshuffle: Experts Urge Caution on Sweeping Changes
stories.summary: The Institute for Government has advised Andy Burnham to resist making sweeping Cabinet changes in his first reshuffle, warning that unnecessarily moving effective ministers could delay government policy.

stories.title: Immigration and Asylum Bill Receives Second Reading in House of Commons
stories.summary: On 13 July 2026, the Immigration and Asylum Bill received its second reading in the House of Commons. Public attention has focused on Part 2 of the bill, which deals with re-departmentalisation of asylum adjudication.

stories.title: Shabana Mahmood Makes Parliamentary Statement on Widdecombe Investigation
stories.summary: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood updated Parliament after counter-terrorism police took over the investigation into the death of Ann Widdecombe, confirming the suspect was 'not known to Prevent'.

stories.title: Speaker Insists MPs Are 'Equal' on Security After Widdecombe Death
stories.summary: The Speaker of the House of Commons insisted that all MPs are treated equally when it comes to security, following criticism from Reform UK in the wake of Ann Widdecombe's murder.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy & internal politics

stories.title: Andy Burnham Secures Labour Leadership with Landslide Support from MPs
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has secured the Labour Party leadership after receiving additional nominations, making it impossible for any other candidate to launch a leadership challenge. He secured backing from 349 Labour MPs.

stories.title: Home Secretary Issues Visa Threat to Deport Rochdale Grooming Gang Leader
stories.summary: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood signalled she will use visa threats to deport the Rochdale grooming gang leader to Pakistan, in a move that has sparked debate on immigration and justice policy.

stories.title: Kemi Badenoch Insists Future Conservative Candidates Must Back Leaving ECHR and Ending Net Zero
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch has made it a requirement that future Conservative candidates must support leaving the European Convention on Human Rights and abandoning the 2050 net zero target, in a shift aimed at making the party more ideologically consistent.

stories.title: YouGov Study: 65% of Conservative Defectors Would Consider Returning
stories.summary: A major YouGov voter study for 2026 finds that immigration concerns and a perception the party hasn't learned from its 2024 defeat are central to ongoing Conservative losses, though 65% of defectors say they would consider returning.

stories.title: Ed Davey Urges Andy Burnham to Change UK Voting System
stories.summary: Liberal Democrat leader Sir Ed Davey is expected to warn that Labour may be 'powerless to do anything' to change the UK's voting system after the next election, urging incoming PM Andy Burnham to act on electoral reform now.

stories.title: YouGov Study Reveals How Lib Dem Voter Coalition Has Shifted Since 2024
stories.summary: A major YouGov 2026 voter study examines how the Liberal Democrat coalition has changed over the last two years, exploring what attracts voters to the party.

stories.title: Ann Widdecombe Murder: Counter-Terrorism Police Take Over Investigation
stories.summary: A 28-year-old man suspected of murdering former Conservative minister and Reform UK spokeswoman Ann Widdecombe, 78, has been re-arrested on terrorism charges. The suspect was not previously known to the Prevent programme.

stories.title: Reform UK Row Erupts Over MP Safety After Widdecombe Death
stories.summary: Reform UK members have called out Parliament over MP security in the wake of Ann Widdecombe's murder, with Nigel Farage accused of using her death for propaganda. Mahmood offered Farage a personal meeting with the Home Office's security unit for high-profile politicians.

stories.title: Secretive Crypto Firm Backed by Farage's Biggest Donor Exposed
stories.summary: BBC News has revealed details of a secretive crypto firm backed by Nigel Farage's biggest financial donor, raising fresh questions about the funding networks behind Reform UK.

stories.title: Nicola Sturgeon Will Not Release Police Statement on SNP Finances
stories.summary: Former SNP leader Nicola Sturgeon's lawyers have confirmed she will not release the written statement she gave to police investigating the party's finances. Sturgeon had been arrested in June 2023 as part of the investigation.

stories.title: SNP Government Bond Plans Labelled a 'Gimmick' by Ross Greer
stories.summary: Scottish Green co-leader Ross Greer has described the SNP's plans to issue government bonds as a 'gimmick', criticising the policy as failing to address an independent Scotland's underlying borrowing needs.

stories.title: Over 80 MPs and MSPs Sign Pledge to Oppose Rosebank Oil Field
stories.summary: A cross-party group of more than 80 MPs and MSPs have signed a pledge opposing the planned Rosebank oil field development, with an SNP MSP breaking party ranks to join the campaign.

stories.title: Burnham Expected to Resist Sweeping Cabinet Reshuffle on Taking Office
stories.summary: Policy analysts are urging incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham to avoid making sweeping Cabinet changes when he enters Downing Street, warning that major reshuffles risk disrupting effective government operations.

stories.title: Makerfield By-Election to Be Called as Burnham Becomes PM
stories.summary: With Andy Burnham leaving his Makerfield seat to become Prime Minister, a by-election will be required. Reform UK has proposed an August date for the by-election, with Farage denying the timing suggestion is a political stunt.

stories.title: Andy Burnham Confirmed as Next Labour Leader With 349 MP Nominations
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is set to become both Labour leader and Prime Minister after securing nominations from 349 Labour MPs — the overwhelming majority of the parliamentary party.

stories.title: Lords Debate Government's Record on Education, Employment and Welfare
stories.summary: Members of the House of Lords pressed the government on its record across education, employment and welfare, alongside debates on regenerative agriculture, violence against retail workers, and the conflict in Sudan.

stories.title: Lords Discuss National Security, Financial Market Reforms and Healthcare
stories.summary: This week's House of Lords business includes discussions covering national security, financial market reforms, healthcare challenges, democratic resilience, social welfare, and employment opportunities.
